The Wolves open up a roster spot by letting go of a player set to make about $50,000 below the league minimum

They now have 3 open roster spots they will need to fill and about $8 million to do it

items.title: Portland was a team I’d mentioned in my trade column a few weeks ago and again on Memphis radio today. There hadn’t been reporting about their interest but I thought it made some sense. Morant’s value was such that they couldn’t get a real “asset” for him, but didn’t give up one.
items.author: Chris Herrington
items.body_excerpt: Portland was a team I’d mentioned in my trade column a few weeks ago and again on Memphis radio today. There hadn’t been reporting about their interest but I thought it made some sense. Morant’s value was such that they couldn’t get a real “asset” for him, but didn’t give up one.

items.author: Blake Murphy
items.body_excerpt:
Raptors are declining the team option on Jonathan Mogbo for 2026-27, am told.

Roundup:

*Shead, Jackson-Davis options picked up
*Mogbo option declined
*Mamukelashvili declined his option

*Martin RFA qualifying offer

*Hepburn re-signed 2-way deal

*Battle deal guarantees July 1

items.title: For those asking why the Hawks would rather decline the option than trade Kuminga...
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
For those asking why the Hawks would rather decline the option than trade Kuminga...

1) They COULD still re-sign him

2) It's not as if they didn't explore the market

3) Kuminga at $24.3 million isn't some overwhelmingly awesome trade chip. Takes two to tango.

items.body_excerpt:
Boston picking up their team options for Neemias Queta and Jordan Walsh makes sense from managing the team salary under the tax and first apron for 2026-27.

Both Queta and Walsh stay on a lower salary for this season and can be extended in-season to start new contracts in 2027-28.

items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:

They only save $2.6 million but create an $8 million trade exception.

Still have plenty of flexibility to take on more money.

They are running out of roster spots for the summer (21 max) and will need to make cuts soon.

items.body_excerpt:
Phoenix Mercury star rookie Jovana Nogić will miss the remainder of the 2026 season due to personal reasons.

Nogić's agent refuted a report that she's leaving because of Ekat/Serbian national team duties.

Goal is for Nogić to return to Phoenix in 2027

items.title: As a general rule, teams don't trade for a player to turn around and trade them again immediately. They just turn it into a three-team trade.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
As a general rule, teams don't trade for a player to turn around and trade them again immediately. They just turn it into a three-team trade.

It does happen on occasion, but very rarely and usually in a very specific circumstance.
